headline: Musk says SpaceX could bring $1 trillion in revenue by 2030
summary: Elon Musk said on Sunday that his rocket company, SpaceX, ‌could bring in $1 trillion in revenue ‌by 2030, making the statement two days after the ​company went public, valuing it at over $2 trillion.  "And I would be surprised if revenue is not greater than $1T in 2031," he ‌wrote on his ⁠social media platform X, replying to journalist and financial commentator Jon Erlichman.  SpaceX ⁠on Friday became the sixth-largest U.S. firm, cementing Musk's status as the world's first ​trillionaire.

content.title: Musk says SpaceX could bring $1 trillion in revenue by 2030
content.summary: Elon Musk said on Sunday that his rocket company, SpaceX, ‌could bring in $1 trillion in revenue ‌by 2030, making the statement two days after the ​company went public, valuing it at over $2 trillion.  "And I would be surprised if revenue is not greater than $1T in 2031," he ‌wrote on his ⁠social media platform X, replying to journalist and financial commentator Jon Erlichman.  SpaceX ⁠on Friday became the sixth-largest U.S. firm, cementing Musk's status as the world's first ​trillionaire.

headline: Israel's El Al Airlines signs internet deal with Elon Musk's Starlink
summary: El Al Israel Airlines has signed a deal with Elon Musk's Starlink to make high-speed internet ‌available across its fleet starting next year, the airline said on ‌Monday.  Financial details of the agreement were not disclosed.  Israel's flag carrier said SpaceX's Starlink service would be ​offered free of additional charge and would allow hundreds of customers to connect simultaneously, including on long-haul flights.

content.title: Israel's El Al Airlines signs internet deal with Elon Musk's Starlink
content.summary: El Al Israel Airlines has signed a deal with Elon Musk's Starlink to make high-speed internet ‌available across its fleet starting next year, the airline said on ‌Monday.  Financial details of the agreement were not disclosed.  Israel's flag carrier said SpaceX's Starlink service would be ​offered free of additional charge and would allow hundreds of customers to connect simultaneously, including on long-haul flights.

headline: Canada's Nuvei to buy Payoneer for $2.75 billion in cross-border payments push
summary: Nuvei will buy cross-border payments firm Payoneer for about $2.75 billion in cash, the companies said on Monday, as ‌the Canadian fintech aims to expand globally.  Nuvei will buy all ‌shares of Payoneer for $7.40 apiece, a premium of about 44% to their last close ​on June 8.  Payoneer has a market capitalization of about $2.26 billion, according to data compiled by LSEG.

content.title: Canada's Nuvei to buy Payoneer for $2.75 billion in cross-border payments push
content.summary: Nuvei will buy cross-border payments firm Payoneer for about $2.75 billion in cash, the companies said on Monday, as ‌the Canadian fintech aims to expand globally.  Nuvei will buy all ‌shares of Payoneer for $7.40 apiece, a premium of about 44% to their last close ​on June 8.  Payoneer has a market capitalization of about $2.26 billion, according to data compiled by LSEG.

headline: Fox to buy streaming pioneer Roku in a $22 billion deal
summary: Fox Corp. has agreed to buy the streaming pioneer Roku in a cash-and-stock deal valued at approximately $22 billion, including debt.  Roku will continue to be run as an open, partner-friendly platform, the companies said Monday, and there appears to be no immediate changes that customers will see.  Fox and Roku said that the combined company will become the third-largest player in U.S. television by share of viewing.

content.title: Fox to buy streaming pioneer Roku in a $22 billion deal
content.summary: Fox Corp. has agreed to buy the streaming pioneer Roku in a cash-and-stock deal valued at approximately $22 billion, including debt.  Roku will continue to be run as an open, partner-friendly platform, the companies said Monday, and there appears to be no immediate changes that customers will see.  Fox and Roku said that the combined company will become the third-largest player in U.S. television by share of viewing.

headline: SharkNinja Fan House Takes Over Queen Street West June 20-21
summary: SharkNinja, in collaboration with Amazon Ads, brings the Fan House to Toronto for one weekend only. Running June 20–21 on Queen Street West, this free, multi-room pop-up is built to feel like the ultimate game-day house: fully stocked, dialed in, and ready for a summer of hosting. The House is a physical extension of the SharkNinjaDigitalFanHouse created by BrandInnovationLab, the creative arm of Amazon Ads.

headline: What Is The Market Really Expecting From PLTR Stock?
summary: At $127.99, Palantir Technologies (PLTR) is being priced to deliver 33.3% revenue growth annually for the next 7 years simply to defend today's 134.3x multiple. That sits below the 67.7% the business is currently growing at, which is the unusual part. The multiple has already priced in a slowdown that has not yet appeared.

content.title: What Is The Market Really Expecting From PLTR Stock?
content.summary: At $127.99, Palantir Technologies (PLTR) is being priced to deliver 33.3% revenue growth annually for the next 7 years simply to defend today's 134.3x multiple. That sits below the 67.7% the business is currently growing at, which is the unusual part. The multiple has already priced in a slowdown that has not yet appeared.

headline: AI Stocks Are Rallying on Iran Peace Deal News, but the Risks Just Got a Lot Bigger
summary: The stock market was overlooking Anthropic’s latest feud with the U.S. government on Monday, which highlights what could be an ongoing problem for tech stocks: government intervention.  Anthropic announced on Friday that it had suspended all access to its newest and most capable models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5.  This came after the government issued an export control directive to suspend access to the models by any foreign national, citing national security concerns.

content.title: AI Stocks Are Rallying on Iran Peace Deal News, but the Risks Just Got a Lot Bigger
content.summary: The stock market was overlooking Anthropic’s latest feud with the U.S. government on Monday, which highlights what could be an ongoing problem for tech stocks: government intervention.  Anthropic announced on Friday that it had suspended all access to its newest and most capable models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5.  This came after the government issued an export control directive to suspend access to the models by any foreign national, citing national security concerns.

headline: Amazon Selects Missouri for $10 Billion Data Center Campus
summary: Amazon today announced plans to invest $10 billion in Missouri to construct a new, state-of-the-art data center campus.
